---
title: "receiving criticism guide"
format: html
---

## Receiving Criticism Guide

Criticism can trigger shame, anger, and urges to shut down. A structured response helps you stay grounded and protect recovery.

You can acknowledge useful feedback without accepting unfair blame. The goal is balanced interpretation, not defensiveness or collapse.

Interpersonal stress is a common relapse risk. Communication habits, emotional reactivity, and body language all affect how quickly conflict escalates and how supported you feel afterward.

When communication becomes clearer and more regulated, stress drops and problem-solving improves. These skills protect recovery by reducing isolation, resentment, and impulsive reactions during difficult interactions.

::: callout
### Instructions
Use a recent example and practice your response sequence.
:::

| What was said | First reaction | Balanced interpretation | My response |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |
| | | | |
| | | | |

### Response Sequence

1. Pause and breathe.
2. Clarify what was meant.
3. Accept any true part.
4. Set boundaries if needed.
